checks.finding: T-001 references AC-001; T-002 references AC-002 through AC-005; T-003 references AC-005 through AC-007, and every identifier exists in acceptance-tests.md.
blockers_format_valid=true: T-001 uses None, T-002 uses T-001, and T-003 uses the valid comma-separated list T-001, T-002.
T-001 covers REQ-001 through REQ-004, T-002 covers REQ-005 through REQ-008 and REQ-010, and T-003 covers REQ-009 and REQ-011; all requirements are covered.
AC-001 is assigned to T-001, AC-002 through AC-005 to T-002, and AC-005 through AC-007 to T-003; every acceptance criterion has task coverage.
Each of T-001, T-002, and T-003 references at least one REQ-NNN in its Requirements field.
No task title or scope is exclusively a test/spec/verify task; each task nevertheless references its applicable AC-NNN identifiers.
In attempt 1 round 1, T-001, T-002, and T-003 each declare Approval: Draft and Status: Planned.
workflow_match_precheck is PASS: T-001 and T-003 pair medium with acceptance-first, while T-002 pairs high with tdd.
The only shared criterion, AC-005, has differentiated scopes: T-002 validates implementation-report handoff fields, while T-003 validates retrospective metrics and release evidence.
dependency-graph.json contains nodes T-001, T-002, and T-003; every blocker reference resolves to one of those nodes.
With blockers_format_valid=true, the graph edges T-002→T-001, T-003→T-001, and T-003→T-002 form an acyclic dependency order.

T-001 coherently addresses model routing, T-002 coherently addresses file-backed context isolation, and T-003 coherently combines iteration reporting with the mandatory publication/rollback work for the same release.

Each task names inspectable TEST identifiers and concrete Bash/PowerShell or repository-validation outcomes; the remaining Done When items identify binary routing, isolation, metadata, hash, and rollback results.
traceability.md cites section anchors that do not exist in the hash-bound security-spec.md and infra-spec.md. Replace them with real anchors or reasoned cross-layer N/A entries.
